questions.question: Which monitoring/logging concept best matches: continuous observation of system and application health metrics?
questions.explanation: Monitoring is defined as: continuous observation of system and application health metrics.

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of 'Monitoring'?
options: component routing and deduplicating monitoring alerts
root cause analysis identifying underlying incident reason
continuous observation of system and application health metrics
capturing timestamped records of system and application events
questions.explanation: Monitoring refers to: continuous observation of system and application health metrics.

questions.question: 'Monitoring' belongs mainly to which monitoring/logging area?
questions.explanation: Monitoring is mainly part of Monitoring Fundamentals.

questions.question: Identify the correct monitoring/logging term-definition mapping.
options: Monitoring -> continuous observation of system and application health metrics
Anomaly Detection -> continuous observation of system and application health metrics
MTTR -> continuous observation of system and application health metrics
Log Rotation -> continuous observation of system and application health metrics
questions.explanation: Only 'Monitoring -> continuous observation of system and application health metrics' is correctly mapped.

questions.question: A Bank SO IT operations requirement states: continuous observation of system and application health metrics. Which concept should be selected?
questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to Monitoring.
